name: React Assessment Template
description: A React + TypeScript template for frontend assessments

files./src/main.tsx:
import React from 'react'
import ReactDOM from 'react-dom/client'
import App from './App'

ReactDOM.createRoot(document.getElementById('root')!).render(
  <React.StrictMode>
    <App />
  </React.StrictMode>,
)
files./tsconfig.json:
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2020",
    "useDefineForClassFields": true,
    "lib": ["ES2020", "DOM", "DOM.Iterable"],
    "module": "ESNext",
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "allowImportingTsExtensions": true,
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "isolatedModules": true,
    "noEmit": true,
    "jsx": "react-jsx",
    "strict": true,
    "noUnusedLocals": true,
    "noUnusedParameters": true,
    "noFallthroughCasesInSwitch": true
  },
  "include": ["src"]
}
files./README.md:
# React Assessment

## Instructions

1. Complete the tasks listed in the assessment panel
2. Edit files in the `src` directory
3. Run `npm install` and `npm run dev` to start the development server
4. Your changes will be automatically tracked

## Getting Started

```bash
npm install
npm run dev
```

Open http://localhost:5173 to view your application.

tasks.title: Create a Counter Component
tasks.description: Build a reusable counter component that increments and decrements
requirements: Component should accept initial value as prop
Should have increment and decrement buttons

tasks.title: Add Form Validation
tasks.description: Create a form with email and password fields with validation
requirements: Password must be at least 8 characters
Show error messages for invalid inputs
Disable submit button when form is invalid
